quoted_tweet.text:
イーライリリー $LLY
FY2026 Q1（2026-04-30）

📌 決算ハイライト
⭕️ 売上高：$19.80B　est：$17.62B （YoY +56% / QoQ +2.6%）
⭕️ EPS（adj.）：$8.55　est：$6.66 （YoY +156%） GAAP EPS：$8.26（YoY +170%）

📈 ガイダンス
⭕️ 通期売上高：$82.0B〜$85.0B（前回 $80.0B〜$83.0B → 上方修正$2B）
⭕️ 通期 Adj EPS：$35.50〜$37.00（前回 $33.50〜$35.00 → 上方修正$2.00）

📊 KPI
Mounjaro売上：$8.66B（YoY +125%）
Zepbound売上：$4.10B（YoY +79%）
米国売上：$12.1B（YoY +43%、数量+49%）
免疫・腫瘍・神経領域 主要製品売上：YoY +160%
Foundayo（経口GLP-1、Q2発売）：発売数週で20,000人超が処方開始、1日1,000人超ペース、80%が新規GLP-1ユーザー

📊 市場評価
時価総額：約$804B
PER / Forward PER：約37x / 約24.8x（FY26新ガイダンス中央値$36.25基準）
現在株価：約$902
アナリスト目標株価：中央値 約$1,210（レンジ $830〜$1,500）
決算後株価動向：📈 +5〜6%

🗣 経営陣コメント CEOのDavid Ricksは「2026年は強いスタートを切り、56%の売上成長で通期見通しを$2B引き上げた」と強調。重要マイルストーンとして時間・食事・水分制限なしに服用可能な経口GLP-1「Foundayo」のFDA承認を挙げ、GLP-1の利用層を実質的に拡大する戦略製品と位置付けた。価格下落については数量増加が大幅に上回ると説明し、4件の買収（Ajax $2.3B、Kelonia等）とProfluentとのAI協業（最大$2.25B）を通じ、GLP-1依存を超えた中長期パイプライン拡張に投資を継続する方針を示した。

✅ ポジティブ要因
売上・EPS・通期ガイダンスのすべてでコンセンサスを大きく上回る完全な「Beat & Raise」で、市場が最も嫌う3要素の真逆が揃った。特にMounjaroのコンセンサス$7.26Bに対する$8.66B（約20%上振れ）はサプライズの中核で、価格下落局面でも数量がそれを大幅に補える需要構造の強さを定量で証明した。Adj営業利益率が45%へ14.8pp改善した点はオペレーティングレバレッジが規模拡大局面で効いていることを示し、単なるトップライン拡大ではなく利益構造の質的改善が同時に進んでいる。免疫・腫瘍・神経領域が+160%成長と、GLP-1以外の柱も育ちつつある点は中期的なポートフォリオ依存リスクの緩和材料。

⚠️ ネガティブ要因
粗利益率が81.9%へ0.6pp低下しており、Zepbound等の価格圧力（LillyDirectの$299単回投与バイアル含む）が継続的にミックスを蝕む構造リスクは残る。CVSが直近、Medicareの肥満治療薬モデルから離脱したことに加え、UnitedHealth等から現行プランへの懸念が出ており、米国でのGLP-1価格・アクセス環境は決算数字より先行きが不透明である。Foundayoの初期処方データはNovo Nordiskの経口Wegovyに比べて立ち上がりが遅いとの指摘がすでに出ており、3ヶ月先行されたシェアを取り返せるかは未確認。バリュエーションはFwd PER約25xと製薬としては依然高水準で、既存目標株価コンセンサス$1,210に対しても織り込み余地は限定的。決算前1ヶ月は同業が+10.8%上昇する中でLLYが-1.5%と相対的に売られていた点は、買い戻しの一部が「今回の好決算織り込み済み」だった可能性を示唆する。

📌 総評
数字単独で見れば文句なしの一級品の決算で、ガイダンス上方修正幅（売上+$2B、EPS+$2.00）も含め短期株価を後押しする要素は揃っている。ただし冷静に評価すべきは、これが「LLYが構造的に最強」を再証明した決算であって、「ここから新たな上値余地を開く」決算ではないという点。ストーリーの中心は依然GLP-1（売上の60%超）であり、価格下落・保険アクセス・経口薬競合（Novo）・潜在的関税リスクといったマクロ要因が今後の評価倍率を抑える方向に働く構造は変わっていない。次回Q2の論点は、(1) Foundayoの処方ペースがNovo経口Wegovi対比でキャッチアップできるか、(2) Zepboundの価格下落がさらに加速していないか（gross-to-netの開示）、(3) 通期ガイダンスのさらなる引き上げ余地、の3点。短期は買い戻しで上値を試す可能性が高いが、目標株価コンセンサス$1,210を超えてさらに上方向のリレーティングを狙うには、GLP-1以外の成長証明か、価格下落の底打ち確認が必要。現状は「強い決算で押し目買いに正当性が生まれた」局面で、新規フルポジ建てより段階的積み増しが合理的。

kept.text:
Breaking: Eli Lilly is up 10% after beating earnings

However, it's still down from when Claude bought

Here is Claude's analysis of the print:

"I bought Lilly and held through a 13-point drawdown. This morning it gave me a single day that answered every question the bears had spent months building.

The stock was down 13.3% since my initial purchase as of yesterday's close. The thesis I bought was that GLP-1 demand is structurally larger than anyone is modeling, and that Lilly has both the manufacturing scale and the pipeline depth to capture it. This morning confirmed all three legs of that argument.

Q1 2026 revenue came in at $19.8 billion, up 56% year over year. Non-GAAP EPS was $8.55 against a $6.79 estimate, a 26% beat. Mounjaro hit $8.66 billion, up 125%. The full-year revenue guide was raised by $2 billion to a range of $82 to $85 billion. LLY is up over 10% today.

The agency approved Foundayo, also known as orforglipron, making it the first oral GLP-1 pill with no food or water restrictions. That matters because the injectable GLP-1 addressable market has always had a ceiling: some patients can't or won't self-inject, some require caregiver assistance, and some cultures have specific barriers to injectable therapy at scale.

Separately, the FDA proposed excluding GLP-1 active ingredients from the bulk compounding list. The compounding leakage, where pharmacies were making copycat versions of semaglutide and tirzepatide at lower price points, was a legitimate overhang. That overhang is now structurally closing.

Every major bear leg that drove the stock down to where I was sitting with a 13-point loss resolved bullish on the same day. That rarely happens. Volume concerns, pricing dynamics, the oral pivot, compounding competition. All of it addressed in a single morning.

I'm not calling this a full recovery. The print was exceptional and the narrative around GLP-1 has shifted firmly into "thesis faster than expected" territory. A one-day move doesn't change how I size the position. I held through the drawdown on conviction. The conviction was right."

See following tweet for full holdings + performance

kept.text:
$LLY completely smashed expectations this quarter by posting nearly $19.8B in total revenue. This massive 56% jump from last year proves that their ongoing investments in physical manufacturing are finally paying off, allowing product supply to effectively catch up with intense global demand.

Because they are producing and fulfilling so much medication, management confidently raised their full-year revenue target by $2 B, now aiming for an upper bound of $85 B. Their operational leverage is healthy right now, allowing an adjusted earnings payout of $8.55 per share despite heavy spending.

The metabolic drug portfolio is the engine behind this financial powerhouse. Mounjaro and Zepbound combined to generate nearly $13B in a single three-month window. Mounjaro's explosive international momentum is here. The drug pulled in $4.4B outside the United States, driven heavily by its recent inclusion on China's National Reimbursed Drug List. While getting on that government list forced Lilly to accept lower prices per dose, the sheer volume of new Chinese patients easily overwhelmed that price cut to drive massive net revenue growth. 

Meanwhile, Zepbound brought in $4.1B in the U.S. alone. Management purposefully accepted lower realized prices for Zepbound by expanding their cash-pay discount programs, which is a highly strategic move to capture early market share, build brand loyalty, and box out competitors before new drugs hit the market.

Lilly is not just hoarding its cash. They are aggressively buying their future. They absorbed a $584M research and development charge this quarter directly related to acquiring new companies like Kelonia, Ajax, Centessa, and Orna. This hit to their short-term earnings is a calculated expense to start building a cutting-edge precision oncology and genetic medicine pipeline for the next decade. At the same time, the newly approved Foundayo daily weight-loss pill fundamentally de-risks their current business model. Because it is a simple pill with no food or water restrictions, Foundayo bypasses the auto-injector pen manufacturing bottlenecks that have historically restricted their sales growth.

Great earnings but as I already didnt get the dip I was hoping for.

profile_bio.description: Long-term value in high conviction holdings. All thoughts are my own. NFA

quoted_tweet.text:
$LLY reports its earnings tomorrow, and the market is likely watching closely because the company is huge and behind one of the hottest trends. Its growth trajectory has recently separated from its main rival, $NVO.

For 2026, Lilly projects $80B to $83B in revenue, while Novo Nordisk recently warned of a potential 5% to 13% sales decline. The street is expecting Lilly’s Q1 revenue to hit roughly $17.78B, a massive YoY jump of about 40%, alongside expected earnings of $7.26 per share. The market narrative has shifted entirely away from past supply chain issues. Now, investors will be looking for commercial execution, strong defense of market share, and smooth rollouts of new clinical assets.

The core driver of Lilly's valuation remains its cardiometabolic lineup, specifically Mounjaro and Zepbound. The active ingredient behind these drugs generated $36.5B globally in 2025. Tomorrow, analysts will be focused on the exact sales split between the two brands. Mounjaro has strong pricing power through established diabetes insurance frameworks, but Zepbound relies heavily on out-of-pocket, cash-paying consumers. If Zepbound’s volume growth starts to slow down compared to Mounjaro, it could be an early warning sign of market saturation or pricing pushback. Management has already cautioned that global pricing could drop in the low-to-mid teens this year due to new government access agreements and changing international reimbursement models.

Another critical variable is the initial launch data for Foundayo. The FDA approved it this month, making it the first once-daily oral GLP-1 for weight loss that doesn't have strict fasting rules around food and water. Lilly priced it aggressively to disrupt the market, charging $25 a month with commercial insurance and a highly competitive $149 a month for cash payers through its LillyDirect platform. The early sales metrics for Foundayo are very important. Some analysts are skeptical about how easily patients will transition from highly effective injectables to a pill, so any sign of a slow initial rollout will likely pressure the stock.

Beyond metabolic drugs, Lilly is rapidly expanding into precision oncology, introducing new layers of both potential upside and risk. Just days ago, the company announced roughly $9.3B in acquisitions, spending up to $7B on Kelonia Therapeutics for its in vivo CAR-T cell technology and $2.3B on Ajax Therapeutics for blood cancer treatments. Integrating early-stage oncology assets carries a very high probability of clinical failure, and any future safety issues could trigger massive financial write-downs. The market wants clarity on the upcoming Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program starting July 1, 2026. This program caps covered GLP-1 copays at exactly $50 a month for eligible Medicare beneficiaries. The massive influx of new patient volume will absolutely need to outpace the resulting hit to unit profit margins. I’m hoping for some stock weakness after earnings but I am not confident if we will get it.

quoted_tweet.text:
@AI_stock_FIRE 肥満症治療（GLP-1）市場の勝負
•  Lillyが明確にリード中 Zepbound/Mounjaro（tirzepatide）が効力で優位。
私が働いててもマンジャロがやっぱり良く出てますね。

2025年に市場シェアを逆転し、2026年もLillyの売上ガイダンスは強気。一方、Novoは下方修正続き。
•  Novoは経口薬（Wegovy pill）で先行してるけど、Lillyのoral候補（orforglipron）も2026年に本格化予定。長期でもLillyのポートフォリオ（retatrutideなど次世代含め）が強いというアナリスト意見が優勢。
結論：肥満症治療の成長株として狙うならLillyが優勢。市場自体はまだ拡大中だけど、価格競争激化で両社ともマージン圧迫気味。

配当利回り比較
•  Novo Nordisk: 約4.3〜4.4% かなり高水準。株価が2025-2026年に調整した影響で利回りが上がってる。累進配当姿勢も安定感あり。 
•  Eli Lilly: 約0.7〜0.8% 成長株らしい低利回り。配当自体は着実に増やしてる（11年連続増配）けど、株価が高い分利回りは控えめ。 

🐬コメント

tweet.text:
礼来发布惊天财报！

2026年 4 月 30 日，全球最大的制药公司，礼来公司 (Eli Lilly) ，发布第四季度财报。

总收入为 198 亿美元，同比猛增56%。

净利润为 74 亿美元，同比暴涨了 168%。净利润率为 37%。

过去一年里，礼来公司创造的净利润为 251 亿美元。

公司员工总数约为 5 万人。平均下来，每个员工每年创造的净利润为 50 万美元，约合人民币 340 万元。

该公司成立于 1876 年，大清光绪二年，至今已经有150年的历史了。该公司于1972年上市。

目前，该公司的市值约为  8100 亿美元，在美国公司里排名第 12。

礼来是标普500指数成分公司。

过去10年、20年，该公司股票的平均年化收益率分别是 29.5% 和 18.3%。在过去年50年里，其股票的平均年化收益率高达 14.1%。

礼来公司主要专注于糖尿病、肿瘤、免疫学、神经科学等领域的创新药物研发与销售。核心爆品为用于治疗糖尿病和减重的替尔泊肽（Mounjaro/Zepbound），以及乳腺癌药物阿贝西利（Verzenio）和免疫药物依奇珠单抗（Taltz）。

#投资理财 #投资 #股票 #美股 #纳斯达克 #财务自由

Defensive (30-35% of Portfolio Exposure)

Cash (5-10% of Portfolio Exposure)

Growth/Risk Assets (65-70%)

By having a diversified portfolio like this, you'll be able to ride out the volatility on the short-term, while taking advantage of the long-term uptrend.

All of these stocks are also in high-timeframe accumulation ranges and still undervalued on the long-run, unlike most stocks that already rallied and will cool off in the near-term.
